skill.name: xget
skill.description: Use when tasks involve Xget URL rewriting, registry/package/container/API acceleration, integrating Xget into Git, download tools, package managers, container builds, AI SDKs, CI/CD, deployment, self-hosting, or adapting commands and config from the live README Use Cases section.
skill.summary: Configures and applies Xget acceleration for package registries, container images, AI inference APIs, and Git repositories with live platform data fetching.
skill.author: xixu-me

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 165 patterns but most are false positives. Backtick detections are JavaScript template literals, not shell execution. PowerShell findings are documentation examples only. Network URLs point to legitimate sources (gitcode.com, GitHub). Environment variable access is standard configuration pattern. One critical heuristics flag dismissed after code review - no credential exfiltration or malicious patterns exist.

low_findings.title: External Network Requests
low_findings.description: Script fetches remote platform catalog and README from gitcode.com and GitHub. URLs are hardcoded but point to legitimate public repositories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed legitimate network fetches for platform data - standard pattern for CLI tools that sync with remote configuration.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access
low_findings.description: Script reads XGET_BASE_URL from process.env for configuration. This is a standard configuration pattern with no evidence of credential exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment variable used only for base URL configuration - no sensitive data extraction or network transmission of secrets.

low_findings.title: File System Operations in Documentation
low_findings.description: REFERENCE.md contains shell examples that write to user profile files (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc). These are documentation examples for users to run, not code executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed these are markdown code blocks showing users how to configure their shells - skill does not execute these automatically.

content.value_statement: Package downloads and container pulls can be slow from distant registries. Xget rewrites URLs to accelerated mirrors, and this skill applies the configuration across your tools automatically.
actual_capabilities: Fetch live platform catalog from Xget repository to get current mirror mappings
Convert upstream URLs to Xget-accelerated paths for registries and APIs
Configure environment variables XGET_BASE_URL temporarily or persistently
Extract and adapt README Use Cases sections for specific tools like Docker, npm, pip
Support multiple package ecosystems: npm, pip, cargo, Docker, Kubernetes, AI providers
Apply configuration changes directly to project files and shell profiles
limitations: Requires Xget base URL to be configured or provided for live deployments
Cannot create new platform mappings - only uses existing catalog entries
Network-dependent for fetching current platform data and README sections
Shell configuration changes require user permissions for profile files

use_cases.title: Accelerate Package Downloads
use_cases.description: Configure npm, pip, or cargo to use Xget mirrors for faster dependency installation in CI/CD pipelines.

use_cases.title: Container Registry Mirroring
use_cases.description: Set up Docker and Kubernetes to pull images through Xget-accelerated registry proxies.

use_cases.title: AI Inference API Routing
use_cases.description: Configure AI SDKs to route inference requests through Xget proxy endpoints with custom base URLs.
use_cases.target_user: Developer integrating AI providers into applications

prompt_templates.title: Basic URL Conversion
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this GitHub URL to use Xget: https://github.com/microsoft/vscode
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick one-off URL rewriting for a single resource

prompt_templates.title: Configure npm Registry
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Xget for npm in my project. My Xget base URL is https://xget.example.com
prompt_templates.scenario: Configuring package manager to use accelerated registry

prompt_templates.title: Docker Registry Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to configure Docker to pull from GHCR through Xget. I need both the docker-compose.yml changes and any daemon config needed.

prompt_templates.title: Full Project Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: My project uses npm, Docker, and GitHub Actions. Configure all three to use Xget with base URL https://xget.mycompany.com. Apply the changes to my actual config files.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive multi-layer integration with file edits

output_examples.input: Configure Xget for pip with base URL https://xget.example.com
output_examples.output:
Created .pip/pip.conf with:
[global]
index-url = https://xget.example.com/pypi/simple/
trusted-host = xget.example.com

Verified configuration by running: pip config list

https://xget.example.com/npm/express

This uses the /npm/ prefix to route through the Xget npm mirror.

best_practices: Always verify the Xget base URL matches your deployment before applying configuration
Test accelerated URLs with a single package before bulk operations
Document which registries are routed through Xget for team awareness
anti_patterns: Do not add trusted-host for Xget endpoints unless required by your package manager
Avoid using placeholder URLs like xget.example.com in production configurations
Do not strip authentication tokens when rewriting API URLs - preserve auth headers

faq.question: What is Xget and how does it accelerate downloads?
faq.answer: Xget is a URL rewriting proxy that routes requests through geographically optimized mirrors. It supports package registries (npm, pip, cargo), container registries (Docker, GHCR), and AI inference APIs.

faq.question: Do I need to install Xget locally to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill configures your existing tools to use Xget endpoints. You need access to an Xget deployment (self-hosted or public) but no local installation.

faq.question: How do I find my Xget base URL?
faq.answer: Your Xget administrator provides the base URL. For self-hosted deployments, it is typically https://your-domain.com. Use the placeholder https://xget.example.com only for documentation templates.

faq.question: Can this skill work without network access?
faq.answer: Limited functionality is available offline using cached platform data, but live features like fetching the current platform catalog require network access to gitcode.com.

faq.question: What happens if a URL conversion returns 404?
faq.answer: A 404 usually means the wrong prefix was used or the upstream platform is not in the Xget catalog. Run the platforms command to verify supported registries.

faq.question: Does Xget work with private registries?
faq.answer: Xget can proxy private registries if your deployment is configured for them. Authentication tokens are preserved during URL rewriting but must be valid for the Xget instance.
